title: cinnamomum percoriaceum
intro: Cinnamomum percoriaceum, also known as Wild Cinnamon or Korintje Cinnamon, is a type of evergreen tree native to Indonesia, belonging to the Lauraceae family. The bark and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cinnamomum percoriaceum include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots are often dried and ground into powder, used in teas, infusions, or as a spice to add flavor and medicinal value to various remedies.
